summary: A solid audience recording of a 1970 show that delivers a smoking Dark Star, St. Stephen and Easy Wind, with surprisingly clear sound for the era.
review: Fans rave about the performance – Dark Star, St. Stephen and Easy Wind are highlighted as standout moments, and Phil Lesh’s bass work gets special praise. The tape is an audience source but is considered a good‑quality capture given the scarcity of soundboard recordings from this period.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are highly favorable (average 4.67 stars) and consistently mention exceptional musical moments; recording quality is praised as good for an audience tape.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note the recording is "good" despite being an audience tap, and comment on the historic lack of soundboard tapes for this era; overall clarity is acceptable for listening.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Listeners describe the set as a classic early‑summer 1970 flow with strong, energetic numbers and seamless transitions that make it a favorite among fans.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil is "banging away" – his bass work on NFA receives explicit praise.
